title: Spatial validation of CAF2 fibroblasts in hepatocellular carcinoma using imaging mass cytometry

platform.vendor: Hyperion Imaging System (Standard BioTools), imaging mass cytometry

findings.executive_summary: Imaging mass cytometry analysis of 17 hepatocellular carcinoma patients across two tissue microarrays (HCC3 and HCC4; 91 ROIs, ~1.27 million cells, 47 channels / 38 antibody targets) aimed at spatially validating CAF2 fibroblast populations using the PACE proximity framework. The completed first chapter confirms data structure and quality, covering segmentation quality, marker panel integrity, normalisation, and cell-type distributions across 29 FlowJo gates. Cell phenotyping uses FuseSOM clustering with scFeatures and treekoR characterisation, and classification models (ClassifyR) relate spatial features to tissue context. Spatial proximity and enrichment analysis of CAF2 fibroblasts (lisaClust, spicyR) and formal differential composition testing between tumour and non-tumour tissue are forthcoming deliverables.
next_steps: Spatial proximity and enrichment analysis of CAF2 fibroblasts relative to tumour and immune populations
Differential composition analysis of cell-type proportions between tumour and non-tumour tissue

embedding.source_text: Spatial validation of CAF2 fibroblasts in hepatocellular carcinoma using imaging mass cytometry | IMC analysis of 17 HCC patients across two TMAs (91 ROIs, ~1.27M cells, 38 antibody targets) to spatially validate CAF2 fibroblasts via the PACE proximity framework; QC and cell phenotyping complete, spatial proximity and differential composition forthcoming. | Methods: image segmentation, cell phenotyping, spatial proximity analysis, differential composition analysis, classification | Data: imaging mass cytometry, spatial proteomics | Disease: hepatocellular carcinoma | Keywords: CAF2 fibroblasts, tumour microenvironment, spatial validation, PACE, proximity analysis | Tools: simpleSeg, cytomapper, FuseSOM, lisaClust, spicyR, scFeatures, treekoR, ClassifyR
